name: research
description: v2.2.0 - Aligns R&D categories with OECD Frascati Manual 2015 and adds the research lifecycle: observation discovery, hypothesis synthesis, prior-work grounding, and approach transition criteria.

interface.shortDescription: Agent-driven R&D protocol for basic/applied/development research with claim and analysis discipline
interface.longDescription: v2.2.0 - A protocol skill for agent-driven R&D across the three OECD Frascati Manual 2015 categories: basic research (new knowledge about underlying foundations without a particular application in view), applied research (new knowledge directed toward a specific practical aim or objective), and experimental development (new or improved products/processes plus additional knowledge). Adds an explicit research lifecycle from observation discovery to hypothesis synthesis, intervention idea, prior-work grounding, plan, execution, analysis, claim, and decision. Research ideation now separates de-anchored raw candidate generation from observation discovery, hypothesis rationale, grounded pruning, and information-gain scoring. Prior work has two roles: material for observations and grounding after candidates exist. Approach transition criteria connect results to NEXT_STEP, REFINE, ADJACENT, PARK, and CLOSE. Requires bounded but sufficient prior-work grounding before every Plan section, with literature/positioning.md recording how work stands on prior work, inherited assumptions, controls/comparators when relevant, known limitations, and claim scope. Enforces shared vocabulary, Divergence checkpoint before execution, PASS/REWORK/INVALID research-review gate before claims/decisions/reports, Toulmin-derived claim structure with explicit alternatives and conditions, analysis discipline, and human-readable reports with required figures. Includes a quant-research extension for time-series and statistically rigorous quantitative R&D. Methods reproducibility is enforced; experiment-level replicability is the agent's discretion, while provenance and claim-to-artifact checks are treated as audit/evidence-integrity controls.
defaultPrompt: Design an R&D plan for this investigation
Help me decide what to do after this experimental result
Write a human-readable report from this plan and runs
